frames: The agent begins in a low, stable crouch with all four legs firmly on the checkered ground.
It lowers its central body closer to the ground, while its front right leg slightly lifts off the surface.
The agent shifts its weight, extending its back left leg and lifting its front right leg further into the air.
Its body remains very low, and it pushes off with its front left leg, causing a slight forward slide.
The agent's body lifts slightly as it extends its front right leg forward, appearing to attempt a step.
It then retracts the front right leg and extends the front left, maintaining a low, crawling posture.
The agent's movements appear somewhat jerky as it repositions its legs, keeping its body close to the surface.
It lifts and extends its front left leg forward, continuing a slow, deliberate crawling motion across the ground.
